engineGuide.h2: Mitsubishi Carisma Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mitsubishi Carisma across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Renault-sourced indirect injection diesel; reliable workhorse but prone to injector seal leaks at higher mileage.
commonFailures: Injector seal failure causing fuel leaks (60k-90k miles)
Turbo oil seal wear on high-mileage units
EGR valve clogging on short-journey cars
entries.cta: Get quotes for F8Q Mitsubishi Carisma engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Common rail DI-D replacement for F8Q; improved efficiency but Denso injector calibration critical for longevity.
commonFailures: Denso injector failure requiring recalibration (70k-100k miles)
Turbo actuator sticking on stop-start driving
Timing belt tensioner wear if not replaced at 60k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for F9Q1 Mitsubishi Carisma engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Higher-output F9Q variant with upgraded injectors; popular in Elegance and Mirage trims, same failure patterns as F9Q1.
commonFailures: Injector coking and calibration drift (75k-100k miles)
Turbo oil seal leak causing blue smoke
DPF-related backpressure on modified units
entries.cta: Get quotes for F9Q2 Mitsubishi Carisma engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Petrol
entries.history: Entry-level SOHC petrol engine; simple design with good reliability but limited power for Carisma's weight.
commonFailures: Oil sludge buildup from extended service intervals
Timing belt wear if not replaced at 60k miles
Ignition coil failure causing misfires
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4G13 Mitsubishi Carisma engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: Most common Carisma petrol engine; balanced performance and reliability, widely available rebuilt units.
commonFailures: Timing belt tensioner failure (60k-80k miles)
Head gasket wear on overheated units
Oil consumption from worn piston rings at high mileage
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4G92 Mitsubishi Carisma engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: Standard 1.8L petrol before GDI; multi-point injection, robust bottom end, good for high-mileage replacement.
commonFailures: Timing belt failure if not preventative replaced
Head gasket failure on overheated engines
Oil pump wear causing low pressure at high mileage
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4G93 SOHC Mitsubishi Carisma engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol (MSX)
entries.history: High-output DOHC variant for MSX trim; performance-oriented with stronger internals but same core failure points.
commonFailures: Head gasket failure under hard driving
Timing belt tensioner wear (replace at 60k miles)
Valve guide wear on high-revving examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4G93 DOHC Mitsubishi Carisma engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol (Direct Injection)
entries.history: Mitsubishi's pioneering GDI technology; excellent fuel economy but carbon buildup on injectors and intake valves is a known issue.
compatibleVariants: 1.8 GDI Equippe, 1.8 GDI Elegance, 1.8 GDI Mirage
commonFailures: GDI injector coking causing rough idle and misfires (50k-80k miles)
Carbon buildup on intake valves requiring specialist cleaning
High-pressure fuel pump wear on neglected units
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4G93 GDI Mitsubishi Carisma engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi Carisma Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi Carisma's 1.9 DI-D diesel (F9Q) and 1.8 GDI petrol (4G93) engines dominate UK replacement enquiries due to injector and carbon buildup issues. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your Carisma.

problems.h4: GDI Injector Coking - Why Mitsubishi Carisma 1.8 GDI Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Carisma 1.8 GDI Equippe, Elegance, Mirage (1997-2004, 4G93 GDI)
problems.rootCause: Direct injection technology causes carbon deposits to build up on injector tips and intake valves, restricting fuel flow and causing misfires, rough idle, and eventual injector failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Ultrasonic cleaning of injectors, intake decarbonising, ECU reset
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; may buy 12-18 months if caught early

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace failed GDI injectors with calibrated units, fuel system flush
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine block is healthy; requires specialist calibration

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 4G93 GDI with upgraded injectors, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage cars; addresses root cause permanently

problems.recommendation: On a Carisma worth £1,500-£3,000, injector cleaning may extend life if symptoms are mild. However, if misfires persist or the engine has over 80k miles, a rebuilt GDI engine with upgraded injectors offers better long-term value and peace of mind.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4G93 GDI engine prices for your Mitsubishi Carisma

problems.h4: F9Q Injector Failure - Why Mitsubishi Carisma 1.9 DI-D Engines Need Replacement
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Carisma 1.9 DI-D Classic, Equippe, Elegance, Mirage (2000-2004, F9Q1 & F9Q2)
problems.rootCause: Denso common rail injectors suffer from internal seal degradation and calibration drift, causing fuel leakage, poor combustion, and eventual injector seizure that can damage the cylinder head.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove injectors, bench test and recalibrate, reinstall with new seals
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if injectors are not physically damaged; short-term fix

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace one failed injector with calibrated unit, code to ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Risk of adjacent injectors failing soon after; not cost-effective long-term

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt F9Q with new injectors, turbo inspection, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best solution for cars over 80k miles; eliminates recurring injector issues

problems.recommendation: F9Q injector failure is rarely isolated. If one injector has failed on a Carisma with over 80k miles, budget for a rebuilt engine - it's more economical than replacing injectors individually and you gain a warranty-backed solution.
problems.cta: Get quotes for F9Q Mitsubishi Carisma diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure - Preventing Catastrophic Damage on Mitsubishi Carisma Engines
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Carisma 1.6 (4G92), 1.8 (4G93), 1.9 TD/DI-D (F8Q, F9Q) across all years
problems.rootCause: Timing belt tensioners wear and belts stretch over time; if the belt snaps, interference engines suffer valve-to-piston contact causing catastrophic cylinder head damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, idlers, water pump; reset service interval
repairOptions.longevity: Essential preventative maintenance; prevents catastrophic failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, replace bent valves, machine surface, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if block is undamaged; labour-intensive with no warranty on repair

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new timing components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution after belt failure; avoids hidden damage risks

problems.recommendation: If your Carisma's timing belt history is unknown or it's over 60k miles since last change, invest in preventative replacement. If the belt has already snapped, a rebuilt engine is almost always better value than head repair - you get warranty coverage and peace of mind.
problems.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Carisma timing belt replacement vs rebuilt engine costs

problems.h4: Head Gasket Failure - Overheating Damage on Mitsubishi Carisma Petrol Engines
problems.rootCause: Coolant system neglect, thermostat failure, or prolonged overheating causes head gasket degradation, allowing coolant and oil to mix and compression loss between cylinders.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace gasket, machine surface if needed, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if block is undamaged and overheating was caught early

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new gaskets, coolant flush, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if overheating was severe or mileage exceeds 100k; avoids hidden damage

problems.recommendation: Head gasket repair can work on low-mileage Carismas with minor overheating. However, if the engine was driven while overheating or has over 100k miles, a rebuilt engine is the safer, more economical choice with warranty protection.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Carisma 4G92 4G93 rebuilt engines

problems.h4: Turbocharger Failure - Boost Loss on Mitsubishi Carisma Diesel Engines
problems.rootCause: Turbo oil feed lines clog with sludge, bearings wear from oil starvation, or actuator mechanisms stick, causing boost loss, blue smoke, and eventual turbo seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean or replace stuck actuator, test boost control, reset ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Only fixes mechanical sticking; doesn't address internal bearing wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit reconditioned turbo, new oil feed lines, boost test
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine block is healthy; ensure oil system is clean first

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt F8Q/F9Q with new turbo, oil system flush, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage diesels; addresses turbo failure and underlying wear

problems.recommendation: Turbo failure on a Carisma diesel often signals broader engine wear. If the car has over 100k miles or shows signs of oil consumption, invest in a rebuilt engine - it includes a new turbo and eliminates the risk of cascading failures.
problems.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Carisma F8Q F9Q rebuilt diesel engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi Carisma
finalCta.paragraph: From GDI injector coking to F9Q diesel failures, the Mitsubishi Carisma's most common engine problems are well-documented and solvable. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mitsubishi Carisma specialists, with every rebuilt engine including upgraded components that address the original failure point. With nationwide delivery, supply & fit options, and a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, you can get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi Carisma Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi Carisma and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Mitsubishi Carisma Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Mitsubishi Carisma is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Mitsubishi Carisma 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.9L diesel powered most Carisma models from 1996-2004, evolving from indirect injection (F8Q) to common rail DI-D (F9Q) for improved efficiency and refinement.
commonFailurePoints: F9Q Denso injector calibration drift; F8Q injector seal leaks; turbo actuator sticking
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Carisma 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Mitsubishi Carisma 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: Introduced in 2001 as an entry-level option, the 1.3L 4G13 offered basic transport with simple SOHC design but limited power for the Carisma's weight.
commonFailurePoints: Oil sludge from extended service intervals; ignition coil failure; timing belt wear
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Carisma 1.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Carisma 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The most common Carisma petrol engine, the 1.6L 4G92 balanced adequate performance with widespread parts availability and straightforward rebuild economics.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt tensioner wear; head gasket failure on overheated units; oil consumption at high mileage
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Carisma 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Carisma 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8L range included standard SOHC/DOHC units and pioneering GDI direct injection; GDI variants require specialist rebuild knowledge due to carbon buildup issues.
commonFailurePoints: intake valve carbon; timing belt failure; head gasket on overheated DOHC units
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Carisma 1.8L petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Mitsubishi Carisma Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Mitsubishi Carisma's UK production run (1995-2004), helping owners identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.description: The Carisma launched with Mitsubishi's proven 4G92 1.6L and 4G93 1.8L petrol engines, establishing the model's initial powertrain lineup.
keyChanges: Mitsubishi Carisma introduced to UK market in saloon and hatchback forms
1.6L 4G92 SOHC and 1.8L 4G93 SOHC petrol engines standard
Classic and Equippe trim levels available from launch
knownFor: Simple SOHC design aids affordability of replacement
Timing belt preventative replacement critical at 60k miles
Good parts availability supports cost-effective rebuilds
years.cta: Get quotes for 1995 Mitsubishi Carisma replacement engines

years.description: Mitsubishi added pioneering GDI direct injection petrol and Renault-sourced F8Q diesel engines, expanding Carisma's appeal to efficiency-focused buyers.
keyChanges: 1.8L 4G93 GDI direct injection engine introduced (Equippe trim)
1.9L F8Q indirect injection diesel added to range
MSX performance variant launched with 4G93 DOHC 140hp engine
mainEngines: 4G93 GDI - 1.8L petrol direct injection (1.8 GDI Equippe)
F8Q - 1.9L diesel indirect injection (1.9 TD Classic/Equippe)
knownFor: GDI technology offers excellent economy but requires specialist rebuild knowledge
F8Q diesel reliable but injector seal leaks common at higher mileage
MSX DOHC engine offers performance but head gasket care needed
years.ticker: Mitsubishi Carisma GDI engine rebuild • Mitsubishi Carisma F8Q injector replacement • Mitsubishi Carisma MSX head gasket

years.description: The facelifted Carisma introduced common rail F9Q DI-D diesel engines, which became the most searched-for replacement units due to injector failure patterns.
keyChanges: F9Q1 (102hp) and F9Q2 (115hp) common rail DI-D diesel engines launched
1.3L 4G13 petrol added as new entry-level option
Elegance and Mirage trims introduced with upgraded equipment
knownFor: F9Q Denso injector failure between 70k-100k miles dominates replacement searches
GDI carbon buildup remains key issue for 1.8L petrol variants
4G92 1.6L offers most cost-effective rebuild option for budget buyers
years.cta: Get quotes for 2000-2004 Mitsubishi Carisma replacement engines
years.ticker: Mitsubishi Carisma F9Q injector failure • Mitsubishi Carisma GDI carbon cleaning • Mitsubishi Carisma 1.6 engine rebuild

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Mitsubishi Carisma was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Mitsubishi Carisma specialists.

faq.h2: Mitsubishi Carisma Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mitsubishi Carisma engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt Mitsubishi Carisma engine prices range from £600 for a 1.3L petrol to £2,200 for a 1.8L GDI, with 1.9L DI-D diesel units averaging £850-£1,900 including a 12-month warranty.
keyPoints: 1.3L/1.6L petrol rebuilds: £600-£1,400 supply only
1.8L petrol and GDI variants: £750-£2,200 depending on specification
1.9L diesel F8Q/F9Q engines: £700-£1,900 with injector calibration included
Supply & fit adds £350-£650 to engine price at partner garages
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Carisma engine replacement prices today

items.question: What engine codes fit my Mitsubishi Carisma?
items.answer: Your Mitsubishi Carisma may have petrol codes 4G13, 4G92 or 4G93 (including GDI), or diesel codes F8Q, F9Q1 or F9Q2 - use our registration checker to confirm exact compatibility.
keyPoints: Petrol: 4G13 (1.3L), 4G92 (1.6L), 4G93 SOHC/DOHC/GDI (1.8L)
Diesel: F8Q (pre-2000 indirect), F9Q1/F9Q2 (2000+ common rail)
Engine code is stamped on block; check service history or VIN decoder
Wrong code fitment can cause ECU and emissions compliance issues
items.cta: Check your Mitsubishi Carisma engine code compatibility now

items.question: How long does Mitsubishi Carisma engine replacement take?
items.answer: Most rebuilt Mitsubishi Carisma engines are dispatched within 3-5 working days, with supply & fit installations typically completed in 1-2 days at partner garages nationwide.
keyPoints: Standard rebuilt engines: 3-5 day dispatch from order confirmation
GDI and F9Q specialist units may require 5-7 days for calibration
Supply & fit appointments scheduled within 7-10 days of engine dispatch
Emergency replacement options available for breakdown scenarios
items.cta: Get a fast quote for your Mitsubishi Carisma engine replacement

items.question: What warranty comes with Mitsubishi Carisma replacement engines?
items.answer: Every rebuilt Mitsubishi Carisma engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour at any UK garage.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on all rebuilt units
Warranty valid UK-wide; claim process managed through supplier network
Reconditioned and used engines may have shorter warranty terms - confirm before purchase
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity post-installation
items.cta: Find Mitsubishi Carisma engines with 12-month warranty coverage

items.question: Can I fit a different engine size in my Mitsubishi Carisma?
items.answer: Engine swaps in the Mitsubishi Carisma are possible but require ECU recalibration, emissions compliance checks and may affect insurance - we recommend like-for-like replacement for reliability.
keyPoints: 1.6L to 1.8L petrol swaps need ECU and wiring harness modifications
Petrol to diesel conversions are complex and rarely cost-effective
Any modification must pass MOT emissions testing for the original fuel type
Insurance must be notified of engine changes to maintain coverage
items.cta: Compare like-for-like Mitsubishi Carisma engine replacement options

items.question: How do I know if my Mitsubishi Carisma needs engine replacement?
items.answer: Key signs your Mitsubishi Carisma needs engine replacement include persistent knocking, blue/white smoke, loss of compression, or catastrophic failure like timing belt snap - get a diagnostic quote to confirm.
keyPoints: Metallic knocking or rattling suggests bearing or piston damage
Blue smoke indicates oil burning from worn rings or valve seals
White smoke with sweet smell signals coolant mixing via head gasket failure
No compression on multiple cylinders usually means replacement is needed
items.cta: Get a diagnostic quote for your Mitsubishi Carisma engine

items.question: Are Mitsubishi Carisma GDI engines reliable after rebuild?
items.answer: Rebuilt Mitsubishi Carisma GDI engines are reliable when specialists address carbon buildup, use calibrated injectors and include intake decarbonising in the rebuild specification.
keyPoints: GDI rebuilds must include injector calibration and intake valve cleaning
Use suppliers experienced with 4G93 GDI technology for best results
Regular fuel system cleaning post-rebuild extends injector life
Avoid cheap rebuilds that skip GDI-specific procedures
items.cta: Compare specialist Mitsubishi Carisma GDI engine rebuilders

items.question: What is the difference between F9Q1 and F9Q2 engines?
items.answer: F9Q1 produces 102hp for Classic/Equippe trims while F9Q2 delivers 115hp for Elegance/Mirage; both share the same Denso injector system and failure patterns.
keyPoints: F9Q1: 102hp, fitted to 1.9 DI-D Classic and Equippe (2000-2004)
F9Q2: 115hp, fitted to 1.9 DI-D Elegance and Mirage (2000-2004)
Both use Denso common rail injectors requiring specialist calibration
Rebuilt units should include new injectors and turbo inspection regardless of variant
items.cta: Get quotes for F9Q1 or F9Q2 Mitsubishi Carisma diesel engines

items.question: Can I get a Mitsubishi Carisma engine supply and fit service?
items.answer: Yes, EngineMarket's network offers Mitsubishi Carisma engine supply & fit packages through vetted UK garages, including delivery, installation, fluids and post-fit testing.
keyPoints: Supply & fit adds £350-£650 to engine price depending on garage location
Includes engine delivery, installation, ECU coding and road test
Warranty validation requires installation by approved partner garage
Mobile fitting available in some regions for breakdown scenarios
items.cta: Book Mitsubishi Carisma engine supply and fit through our network

items.question: How do I choose between used, rebuilt and reconditioned Mitsubishi Carisma engines?
items.answer: Choose used Mitsubishi Carisma engines for budget short-term fixes, rebuilt for best value with warranty protection, or reconditioned for premium longevity on high-value variants.
keyPoints: Used: £450-£1,200, variable condition, shorter warranty, verify compression first
Rebuilt: £600-£2,200, upgraded internals, 12-month warranty, best overall value
Reconditioned: £750-£1,900, thorough inspection and part replacement, balanced option
Always match engine code and variant; avoid cheapest quote without warranty
items.cta: Compare all Mitsubishi Carisma engine types and prices now
